entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A sustainable intensification paradigm for food production involving minimal soil disturbance, mulching, and crop rotation

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the study's observations and farming systems are located

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The measure of agricultural productivity, specifically the amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL TILLAGE
entities.description: Traditional farming systems involving regular soil disturbance

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A major crop species analyzed for yield response under conservation agriculture

entities.name: MULCHING
entities.description: The practice of covering soil with organic or inorganic material to conserve moisture and suppress weeds

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same piece of land

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL
entities.description: An environmental condition characterized by limited precipitation

entities.name: HERBICIDES
entities.description: Chemical substances used to kill unwanted plants/weeds

entities.name: SOIL CONSERVATION
entities.description: The prevention of soil loss from erosion or reduced fertility

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY
entities.description: The state of being without reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: META-ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical procedure for combining data from multiple studies to identify trends
